missionary.lesson1.title: The Restoration of the Gospel of Jesus Christ
missionary.lesson1.doctrineFocus: Help the person being taught recognize that God speaks today through prophets and that the Restoration is evidence that Jesus Christ leads His Church.
missionary.lesson1.overview: It teaches that the Church of Jesus Christ was established, fell into apostasy, and was restored to its fullness through the Prophet Joseph Smith. Always connect this message with the invitation to act: pray, read the Book of Mormon, and attend Church.
missionary.lesson1.keyPoints.0: God is our loving Heavenly Father and continues to reveal His will today.
missionary.lesson1.keyPoints.1: Jesus Christ organized His Church with prophets, apostles, and priesthood authority.
missionary.lesson1.keyPoints.2: There was a Great Apostasy: authority was lost and doctrines and ordinances were altered.
missionary.lesson1.keyPoints.3: Through Joseph Smith, God restored the priesthood, His Church, and the Book of Mormon as evidence of this work.
missionary.lesson1.keyScriptures.0: Amos 3:7 – The Lord reveals His secret to His prophets.
missionary.lesson1.keyScriptures.1: 2 Thessalonians 2:3 – An apostasy would come before the coming of the Lord.
missionary.lesson1.prophetQuotes.0: President Russell M. Nelson taught that "the Restoration is a process, not an event." It helps the investigator see that he too is part of that process by seeking, asking, and following the truth.
missionary.lesson1.prophetQuotes.1: President Gordon B. Hinckley testified: "We do not find truth by chance; we receive it when we seek it with real intention."
missionary.lesson1.questions.0: What does the First Vision teach you about how God views your doubts and questions?
missionary.lesson1.questions.1: If God called prophets in ancient times, why would it be important to have a prophet today?
missionary.lesson1.questions.2: What would you be willing to do if you learned that Joseph Smith really was a prophet of God?
missionary.lesson1.commitments.0: Read the account of the First Vision in Joseph Smith—History 1:11–20 and share what you felt.
missionary.lesson1.commitments.1: Pray to know if Joseph Smith was a prophet and if the Book of Mormon is the word of God.
missionary.lesson1.commitments.2: Attend Church next Sunday to experience how the restored Church functions.
missionary.lesson1.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 3, lesson 1; emphasis on the invitation to read, meditate and pray on the Book of Mormon.
missionary.lesson1.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Reverence when speaking the name of Jesus Christ and respect when referring to other faiths.
missionary.lesson2.title: The Plan of Salvation
missionary.lesson2.doctrineFocus: Help the investigator see his or her life within God's eternal plan and understand that the Atonement of Jesus Christ is the center of the plan.
missionary.lesson2.overview: It presents the divine origin of man, the purpose of life, the reality of death and the hope of resurrection and the degrees of glory. It should naturally lead to talking about faith, repentance, baptism and persevering to the end.
missionary.lesson2.keyPoints.0: We lived with God in the premortal life as His spiritual children.
missionary.lesson2.keyPoints.1: Earthly life is a time of testing to become more like Christ.
missionary.lesson2.keyPoints.2: The Fall of Adam and Eve was part of the plan: it brought opposition, mortality, and the need for a Redeemer.
missionary.lesson2.keyPoints.3: The Atonement of Jesus Christ defeats sin and death and makes resurrection and repentance possible.
missionary.lesson2.keyPoints.4: God has prepared degrees of glory according to our faith, works, and covenants.
missionary.lesson2.keyScriptures.0: 2 Nephi 2:25 – Men exist that they may have joy.
missionary.lesson2.keyScriptures.1: Alma 34:32 – This life is the time to prepare for God.
missionary.lesson2.keyScriptures.2: 1 Corinthians 15:20–22 – In Christ all will be made alive.
missionary.lesson2.prophetQuotes.0: President Russell M. Nelson taught that understanding the plan of salvation “changes the way we look at everything”—death, trials, decisions, and even the future.
missionary.lesson2.prophetQuotes.1: Elder D. Todd Christofferson said that Christ's Atonement "not only cleanses us, but strengthens us to become."
missionary.lesson2.questions.0: How does knowing that you lived with God before you came to earth change your perspective?
missionary.lesson2.questions.1: What trials in your life have been difficult but have helped you get closer to God?
missionary.lesson2.questions.2: What does it mean to you to know that Jesus Christ defeated death for you personally?
missionary.lesson2.commitments.0: Read 2 Nephi 2 and mark what you learn about the purpose of life.
missionary.lesson2.commitments.1: Pray for help to face a specific trial with greater trust in the Savior.
missionary.lesson2.commitments.2: Accept a tentative date for baptism as part of following God's plan.
missionary.lesson2.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 3, lesson 2. Connect the plan with the doctrine of Christ and the invitation to baptism.
missionary.lesson2.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Follow safety and device-use guidelines when teaching about life after death or sharing media.
missionary.lesson3.title: The Gospel of Jesus Christ
missionary.lesson3.doctrineFocus: Present the doctrine of Christ as the complete way: faith, repentance, baptism, reception of the Holy Spirit and persevering to the end.
missionary.lesson3.overview: This lesson should focus on inviting people to act: exercise faith, repent, prepare for baptism, and receive the Holy Ghost. It is not just information; it is a call to follow Christ.
missionary.lesson3.keyPoints.0: True faith in Christ leads to action and change.
missionary.lesson3.keyPoints.1: Repentance is an ongoing process of returning to God, not a one-time event.
missionary.lesson3.keyPoints.2: Baptism by immersion and by authority is the gateway to the path of discipleship.
missionary.lesson3.keyPoints.3: The gift of the Holy Spirit guides, comforts and sanctifies us.
missionary.lesson3.keyPoints.4: Persevering to the end means continuing to live the doctrine of Christ every day.
missionary.lesson3.keyScriptures.1: Acts 2:37–38 – What shall we do? Repent and be baptized.
missionary.lesson3.prophetQuotes.0: President Dieter F. Uchtdorf taught that repentance "is not punishment; it is hope." Teach them that the doctrine of Christ always leads to hope.
missionary.lesson3.prophetQuotes.1: Elder Jeffrey R. Holland testified: “There is no life so ruined that it cannot be rebuilt through the Atonement of Jesus Christ.”
missionary.lesson3.questions.0: What changes do you feel the Savior is inviting you to make in your life right now?
missionary.lesson3.questions.1: What would it mean for you to make a covenant with God through baptism?
missionary.lesson3.questions.2: How would having the constant company of the Holy Spirit help you in daily decisions?
missionary.lesson3.commitments.0: Accept or maintain a specific date for baptism.
missionary.lesson3.commitments.1: Make a daily prayer of specific repentance this week.
missionary.lesson3.commitments.2: Attend sacrament meeting to observe and prepare to renew covenants.
missionary.lesson3.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 3, lesson 3; chap. 11 about inviting people to be baptized.
missionary.lesson3.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Standards for baptismal interviews and worthiness for the ordinance.
missionary.lesson4.title: The Commandments
missionary.lesson4.doctrineFocus: Show that the commandments are evidence of God's love and part of the covenant to follow Christ.
missionary.lesson4.overview: Do not present the commandments as a list of rules, but as a path to freedom and protection. Connect each commandment with specific blessings and with the doctrine of Christ.
missionary.lesson4.keyPoints.0: Keeping the commandments is evidence of our love for God.
missionary.lesson4.keyPoints.1: The Ten Commandments and the law of chastity protect families and society.
missionary.lesson4.keyPoints.2: The Word of Wisdom and the law of tithing are acts of faith that bring spiritual power.
missionary.lesson4.keyPoints.3: Service and love of neighbor reflect the character of Christ.
missionary.lesson4.keyScriptures.0: John 14:15 – If you love me, keep my commandments.
missionary.lesson4.keyScriptures.1: Mosiah 2:41 – Blessings of keeping the commandments.
missionary.lesson4.prophetQuotes.0: President Thomas S. Monson said, "The commandments are not chains, they are wings." Use it to help see the commandments as protection.
missionary.lesson4.prophetQuotes.1: President Nelson has repeated: "The Lord loves effort." Keeping commandments requires constant effort.
missionary.lesson4.questions.0: Which commandment do you find most difficult to keep and why?
missionary.lesson4.questions.1: What blessings would you like to receive by better keeping the commandments?
missionary.lesson4.questions.2: What small change could you start today?
missionary.lesson4.commitments.0: Identify a specific commandment and make a concrete plan to best keep it this week.
missionary.lesson4.commitments.1: Begin to live the Word of Wisdom (if applicable) and allow missionaries to accompany you in the process.
missionary.lesson4.commitments.2: Prepare to pay tithing after baptism as part of your covenant.
missionary.lesson4.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 3, lesson 4; chap. 7 on learning the language of the heart to teach sensible commandments.
missionary.lesson4.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Avoid inappropriate conversations when teaching chastity or discussing media standards.
missionary.lesson5.title: Laws and Ordinances
missionary.lesson5.doctrineFocus: Connect the ordinances with the power of the Atonement and show how the laws of the gospel prepare us to make and keep covenants.
missionary.lesson5.overview: Explains that God works through eternal laws and sacred ordinances. These are not empty rituals, but rather points of access to the power of Christ.
missionary.lesson5.keyPoints.0: Ordinances are performed by priesthood authority and are tied to covenants.
missionary.lesson5.keyPoints.1: Baptism, confirmation, and the sacrament are essential ordinances of entry and renewal.
missionary.lesson5.keyPoints.2: Temple and sealing ordinances unite families for eternity.
missionary.lesson5.keyPoints.3: God offers these ordinances also to the dead through vicarious work.
missionary.lesson5.keyScriptures.0: D&C 84:20 – In the ordinances the power of divinity is manifested.
missionary.lesson5.keyScriptures.1: John 3:5 – Born of water and the Spirit.
missionary.lesson5.prophetQuotes.0: President Boyd K. Packer taught that ordinances are “the channels through which the power of the Atonement flows” into our lives.
missionary.lesson5.prophetQuotes.1: President Nelson encouraged a focus on the temple, saying, “The temple is the ultimate goal of all we do in the Church.”
missionary.lesson5.questions.0: What difference does it make to know that God has prepared specific ordinances to bless you?
missionary.lesson5.questions.1: How does knowing that ordinances can be made for ancestors change your view of death?
missionary.lesson5.questions.2: Do you want to prepare to receive all the ordinances God has for you and your family?
missionary.lesson5.commitments.0: Attend Church regularly to partake of the sacrament.
missionary.lesson5.commitments.1: Make a list of names of deceased family members (if applicable) and pray about them.
missionary.lesson5.commitments.2: Continue preparing for baptism and confirmation as the first set of ordinances.
missionary.lesson5.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 3, lesson 5; chap. 12 on temple work and family history.
missionary.lesson5.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Show reverence when teaching about the temple and avoid sharing sacred details outside it.
missionary.lesson6.title: Preparation for Baptism
missionary.lesson6.doctrineFocus: Ensure that the investigator understands the baptismal covenant and is spiritually ready to do so.
missionary.lesson6.overview: This lesson is very practical: it assesses preparation, reinforces doctrine, and clarifies covenant expectations. It must be done close to the baptismal date.
missionary.lesson6.keyPoints.0: Baptism is a saving ordinance and covenant with God, not just a social step.
missionary.lesson6.keyPoints.1: It requires faith in Jesus Christ, repentance, willingness to keep commandments and belong to the Church.
missionary.lesson6.keyPoints.2: The baptismal interview confirms preparation and strengthens the person's confidence.
missionary.lesson6.keyPoints.3: After baptism, confirmation and the gift of the Holy Spirit are essential.
missionary.lesson6.keyScriptures.1: Romans 6:3–4 – Buried with Christ through baptism.
missionary.lesson6.prophetQuotes.0: President Nelson has emphasized that the covenants "bind us to Christ" and that we need to remember them frequently.
missionary.lesson6.prophetQuotes.1: Elder David A. Bednar taught that covenants and ordinances “open the channel” to receive the power of the Atonement.
missionary.lesson6.questions.0: What do you understand you are promising God in baptism?
missionary.lesson6.questions.1: Is there any commandment or aspect of the Church that still concerns you before you are baptized?
missionary.lesson6.questions.2: How do you feel about having a set date for your baptism?
missionary.lesson6.commitments.0: Review the baptismal interview questions together and clarify any questions.
missionary.lesson6.commitments.1: Attend at least one additional Come, Follow Me or Sunday School class before baptism.
missionary.lesson6.commitments.2: Invite family or friends to attend your baptismal service.
missionary.lesson6.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 12 on baptism and confirmation; appendices with the baptismal interview.
missionary.lesson6.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Standards for reverence in baptismal services and coordination with ward or branch leaders.
missionary.lesson7.title: Preparation for the Temple
missionary.lesson7.doctrineFocus: Help the new convert or advanced investigator see the temple as a natural goal of the covenants.
missionary.lesson7.overview: This lesson focuses on the temple, recommend, ordinances for the dead, and continued worship. It must be taught with reverence and clarity, without going into sacred details.
missionary.lesson7.keyPoints.0: The temple is the house of the Lord and is centered on Jesus Christ and His Atonement.
missionary.lesson7.keyPoints.1: Receiving a temple recommend is evidence of worthiness and desire to keep covenants.
missionary.lesson7.keyPoints.2: Temple ordinances bless both the living and the dead.
missionary.lesson7.keyPoints.3: Regular temple worship brings peace, revelation, and spiritual protection.
missionary.lesson7.keyScriptures.0: Isaiah 2:2–3 – Out of Zion will come the law.
missionary.lesson7.keyScriptures.1: D&C 124:39 – Ordinances for the living and the dead.
missionary.lesson7.prophetQuotes.0: President Nelson has invited members to be “worthy of a current temple recommend” and to see it as a symbol of their Christ-centered life.
missionary.lesson7.prophetQuotes.1: President Howard W. Hunter declared: "Make the temple the great goal of your life."
missionary.lesson7.questions.0: How do you feel when you think about entering the house of the Lord one day?
missionary.lesson7.questions.1: Is there any aspect of the temple that gives you doubts or fears that we can clarify?
missionary.lesson7.questions.2: Who would you like to bless by performing ordinances for your ancestors?
missionary.lesson7.commitments.0: Remain worthy to receive a temple recommend as soon as your leader deems it appropriate.
missionary.lesson7.commitments.1: Begin collecting names and stories of deceased family members (if possible).
missionary.lesson7.commitments.2: Attend the temple to perform baptisms for the dead when given the opportunity.
missionary.lesson7.pmgRefs: Preach My Gospel, chap. 3, lesson 5 (part of vicarious work and temple); chap. 13 on retaining new converts.
missionary.lesson7.handbookRefs: Missionary Handbook: Standards of personal worthiness and temple recommend preparation.
